articles.title: Bruins fans will need to wait a little longer for James Hagens’s NHL debut
articles.description:
"I’m not going to throw that kid in and have him fail. I don’t want to do that."

The post Bruins fans will need to wait a little longer for James Hagens’s NHL debut appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: James Hagens's NHL debut for the Bruins is delayed to ensure his readiness.
Bruins coach emphasizes player development, prioritizing Hagens's long-term success over immediate play.
Fans will have to wait longer for the highly anticipated debut of the young prospect.

articles.title: Boston billionaires, historic figures land on Forbes’ ‘self-made’ lists
articles.description:
The rankings consider financial success, obstacles overcome, and long-term impact.

The post Boston billionaires, historic figures land on Forbes’ ‘self-made’ lists appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston billionaires featured on Forbes' 'self-made' lists highlight local economic success.
Notable figures include John Fish and Abigail Johnson, showcasing diverse industries.
Recognition boosts Boston's reputation as a hub for entrepreneurship and innovation.

articles.title: What you need to know about the 2026 Boston Marathon
articles.description:
Everything to know about the 130th Boston Marathon: schedule, how to watch, and more.

The post What you need to know about the 2026 Boston Marathon appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: The 130th Boston Marathon is scheduled for April 20, 2026.
Expect over 30,000 runners participating, impacting local traffic and businesses.
Residents can watch the race live along the route for community engagement.

articles.title: Report: Mass. drivers have paid more than $1 million extra at the pump since the Iran war began
articles.description:
A new U.S. Congress Joint Economic Committee analysis finds fuel costs surged for state motorists and home heating after the conflict started in late February.

The post Report: Mass. drivers have paid more than $1 million extra at the pump since the Iran war began appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Massachusetts drivers paid over $1 million extra at the pump since late February 2023.
Fuel costs surged significantly due to the ongoing Iran conflict, affecting local motorists.
Home heating expenses also increased, impacting Boston residents' budgets this winter.
